title: [Bug]: Navigation on different cameras screen causes a dark screen
author: Egorchik4
body:
### Bug Type

Camera preview

### Device

All

### Platform

Android

### Platform Version

All

### Library Version

v1.0.2

### Describe the bug

When I need navigate from screen with camera to another screen with camera, second camera not working.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use androidx.navigation.compose library for navigation.
2. Create two Compose function with cameras in navigation graph.
3. Navigate with compose navigation between camearas screen.

### Expected behavior

Camera correct working

### Reproducibility

Always

title: [Bug]: rememberCameraSession with Hilt DI
author: Egorchik4
body: ### Bug Type Build/Setup ### Device All ### Platform Android ### Platform Version All ### Library Version v1.0.1 ### Describe the bug Crash app ### Steps to reproduce 1) Use Hilt dependency injection library 2) Build project 3) Open camera ### Expected behavior Not crash ### Reproducibility Always ### Issue logs ```shell FATAL EXCEPTION: main Process: youdrive.today.dev, PID: 30756 java.lang.ClassCastException: dagger.hilt.android.internal.managers.ViewComponentManager$FragmentContextWrapper cannot be cast to androidx.lifecycle.LifecycleOwner at com.ujizin.camposer.internal.core.camerax.CameraXControllerWrapper.(CameraXControllerWrapper.kt:39) at com.ujizin.camposer.session.CameraSession.(CameraSession.android.kt:70) at com.ujizin.camposer.session.CameraSessionState_androidKt.rememberCameraSession(CameraSessionState.android.kt:21) ``` ### Additional context Hilt modifiers Context class and use ContextWrapper instead. LocalContext.current method in compose function return "custom" Context because of theme we have crash with cast exception. How fix it ? Replace one line in rememberCameraSession with `val context = (LocalContext.current as ContextWrapper).baseContext` instead `val context = LocalContext.current` This is the wrong method: This correct method: ### Checklist - [x] I checked existing issues to avoid creating a duplicate. - [x] I can provide more debugging details if needed. - [x] I am willing to submit a PR for this issue.

title: Feature request: take 360-degree photo
author: BDVGitHub
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It is currently not possible to generate [360-degree photo](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/VR_photography) with this library.
To do something like this [park bertin 360 degree photo](http://www.360cities.net/image/park-bertin-in-autumn-20111105#247.94,-1.77,70.0) and maybe use these pictures for VR with [Immersive Web Developer](https://immersiveweb.dev/)

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Use [Image stitching](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/image-stitching-with-opencv/) to creat a 360 degree photo with [OpenCV](https://opencv.org/). By instructing the user how they need to move there phone while taking photos and afterwards stitch these fotos together to generate an 360 degree photo.

title: Feature request: Choose which camera lens to use
author: BDVGitHub
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
On some phones, you have more than 1 lens on the back. Each lens is designed for a different mode: Wide, Ultra-Wide, or Telephoto.
Now you can only choose between front and back camera.

Samsung Galaxy A35 cameras
![afbeelding](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a3797210-8f30-4d33-86c2-b8493ebd7164)

Or the Samsung Galaxy A53 quad camera phone
![Screenshot_20250101_092818_Firefox](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d5f8514-6417-48ea-92e2-57625d5db5dc)


**Describe the solution you'd like**
Is it possible to choose which lens to use to take a picture or film in case there are multiple lenses on the back of the phone?

Use https://developer.android.com/reference/androidx/camera/core/CameraSelector.Builder#setPhysicalCameraId(java.lang.String)

title: CameraPreview extends over parent size on API level 24
author: Walmann
body:
- Device: Emulator, Hardware profile "Small Phone" in Android studio.
- Android version: 7 API level 24
- Library version: 0.4.0

**Describe the bug**

The camera preview does not respect size constrains.
I have set the height with .height(), .weight(), .fillMaxHeight(). But nothing works. The only thing that seems to affect it is padding, but that will also affect all other screen sizes and API levels.

I have tested with API level 24 and 30 with the same screensize, and it only happens on level 24. 

**To Reproduce**

- Create "small phone" with API level 24
- Create a CameraPreview

**Screenshots**

![smallPhone](https://github.com/ujizin/Camposer/assets/6574240/e5e33a53-8017-4de5-b2e9-9b8a658aa84e)
